rows.title: Cole and the Chameleon Try to Sing the Loudest Possible Song
paragraphs: At the hallway, Cole told the chameleon the plan. The plan was simple: sing the loudest possible song. The chameleon nodded immediately. It was on.
The backpack troll stood between Cole and victory. Cole felt brave about it. The backpack troll did not look like it was going to move on its own.

Cole walked up to the backpack troll in full brave mode. Not asking permission. Not apologizing. Just brave. The backpack troll had not prepared for brave. The backpack troll took a small step back. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little scarlet.

"stinky bananas!" said Cole. The backpack troll heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole sang the loudest song. The chameleon applauded the backpack troll, because manners. Cole shuffled one more time, just to make a point. "squonk," went somebody. Nobody asked who. Somebody had brought ice cream. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The chameleon kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole and the Hamster Take the Stage
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the elevator. The co-star was the hamster. The crucial prop was a wobbly telescope.
Right on cue, the wobbly telescope did the WRONG thing. It launched itself off the table, bounced once, and landed in the hamster's lap. The hamster looked at Cole like, "Well now what?"

Cole took a breath, leapt forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "sock puppet!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. Everything in the room had picked up a faint scarlet tint. Later someone would swear they heard "booger cloud" in the rafters.

"sock puppet!" yelled Cole, pointing at the broken wobbly telescope. The wobbly telescope twitched. Twitched again. Then it did exactly what it had refused to do five minutes ago. Pillows lost it. Cole kept feeling confused about the whole thing. Somebody had brought donuts. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded. Off to the side, the knight was processing all of this with visible difficulty.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the hamster. The show would never be performed exactly that way again. That was kind of the point.

rows.title: How Cole Outsmarted the Stone Giant
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the living room. Cole and the tiger were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The stone giant declared the spaghetti forbidden. Cole felt ridiculously cheerful about this development. Possibly more ridiculously cheerful than the stone giant had bargained for.

Cole zoomed sideways while holding the backpack zipper. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. The tomato red thing was happening again, whatever it was.

Cole held the spaghetti up like a trophy. The stone giant could only watch. "mister floof!" yelled Cole. Both things were now true at once. A distant "pickle crown" echoed from somewhere, possibly a memory.
The tiger watched the loophole work and looked impressed. The tiger was going to try this later. Cole had taught the tiger something brand new. Bedtime: earned.

rows.title: Cole and the Duckling Try to Find the Best Possible Snack
paragraphs: Cole woke up with a plan. Today, at the animal shelter, Cole was going to find the best possible snack. The duckling was in. The duckling was always in.
There was just one problem: a alien stood between Cole and the goal. "Nope," said the alien. "Not today. Not find the best possible snack. Not on my watch."

And then Cole did the most sunset orange thing imaginable: walked right past the alien while pretending to be a sunset orange traffic cone. The alien watched, confused. The plan was working. A "magnet shoe" hung in the air for a second longer than expected.

"sneezy-pants!" said Cole. The alien heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole found the best possible snack. The duckling applauded the alien, because manners. For exactly two seconds, Cole tiptoed like it was a job. Throughout, Cole stayed extra brave. Steadily extra brave. Somebody had brought cereal. Nobody knew when. Nobody minded.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The duckling kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

rows.title: Cole vs the Rule
paragraphs: It was a normal day at the art room. Cole and the chameleon were minding their own business. The day was, technically, fine. So far.
The sock monster declared the smoothie forbidden. Cole felt dramatic about this development. Possibly more dramatic than the sock monster had bargained for.
Cole spun sideways while holding the sticker sheet. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back.

And just like that, Cole got the smoothie anyway. The sock monster sighed. "Captain Noodle!" yelled Cole. The rule was still a rule, but Cole had won this round. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little purple. "burp bubble," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
Heading home, Cole explained the loophole to the chameleon one more time. The chameleon was nodding seriously, clearly planning to deploy it later. Knowledge: transferred.

"fwoosh!" said Cole. The knight heard it, made a small noise, and stepped aside in a way that looked rehearsed. Cole built the perfect hideout. The koala applauded the knight, because manners. For exactly two seconds, Cole spun like it was a job. some donuts sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
On the way home, Cole replayed the moment everything turned around. The koala kept bouncing along, already ready for whatever was next. Win logged. Onward.

The talking horse declared the milkshake forbidden. Cole felt snacky about this development. Possibly more snacky than the talking horse had bargained for.

Cole belly-flopped sideways while holding the foam finger. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back. And one of them, very quietly, said "grumblepoof."

And just like that, Cole got the milkshake anyway. The talking horse sighed. "blorp!" yelled Cole. The rule was still a rule, but Cole had won this round. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little mint green.
